required_complexity_patterns.notes: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell
Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root
Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only
Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click
Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs

Ashby yes no

Are you authorized to work in this country?

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Apply form hosted inside iframe or srcdoc iframe shell Required: Fields inside a shadow DOM host or declarative shadow root Required: Inputs laid out in table cells, not flex card stacks only Required: Fields inside details/summary or hidden until click Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Reference DOM (Conditional radio reveal): Selecting Yes on sponsorship reveals follow-up textarea Reference DOM (Triple nested fieldsets): Fieldset inside fieldset inside fieldset with legends at each level Reference DOM (Ashby yes no buttons): Ashby-inspired yes/no as two styled buttons instead of native radio Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: iframe-apply, shadow-host, table-layout, details-accordion, pill-yes-no. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: sidebar + main split apply layout. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Conditional radio reveal; Triple nested fieldsets; Ashby yes no buttons.
